notes: I've tried every fix and tinkering step I could find from trying different launch options to different versions of proton, reinstalling the game, verifying the files. I've seen this exact issue mentioned in a couple other places but found no solution or even any ideas as to what the issue actually is. Intially I thought maybe it was an Nvidia and wayland issue since I'm running hyprland on wayland but I found a post of someone having the same issue with an AMD card. The only similarity between me and both the other posts I found was that they all were also running wayland but it was only 2 posts and theres so many other people also running wayland that are having no issues.

title: Baldur's Gate 3

notes:
If you're in the same boat, please read on - this might actually help you.

I was playing the game a lot, but one day, the game simply didn't start. Only the button in steam was changing colors. There was nothing in the proton log. I've tried every tip I've found on the Internet - reinstalling the game, various launch options, proton versions, flatpak version of steam, installing various libraries using protontrick, different video driver, I've even tried different distributions. After many days of trying to run the game again, I've simply installed wine from the repository and used it to run the binary installed by Steam. It runs flawlessly. No more achievements or cloud saves, but I can finally play again. 
Yes, I can run the game, but I cannot start it via Steam any more.  I think it's not a problem of the Proton, but it seems something is rotten in the Steam application.
title: Baldur's Gate 3

notes: Downgrade proton to 8 or 9

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT (radeonsi, navi10, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.57, 6.8.0-59-generic)
notes:
After installing it the game runs fine. Any following attempts to run the game either fail/freeze or run but no sound, or only menu and dialogue sound. Using Proton Experimental worked for a bit, if it had no audio I just had to exit and run it again and it'd work as expected. Now though it either: crashes immediately upon running, "starts" and freezes (all process stats stuck forever in N/A), or it runs without any audio or sound. Before it would run with only menu audio and dialogue, now it's not even that much. The only consistent "fix" I have managed is reinstalling, but I don't think anyone wants to reinstall a 100+ GB game before every every session.

Please help.
title: Baldur's Gate 3
